talk_metadata.talk_title: Implementation gaps and the TUBAWISP-2 trial for risk‑reducing salpingectomy
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the threat of future high‑grade serous peritoneal cancer is still considerable, upwards of 30% at 10 years"
Speaker said: "adoption of opportunistic salpingectomy at the time of inpatient hysterectomy has stalled out at around 60% since 2018"

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Risk‑reducing salpingectomy vs standard risk‑reducing salpingo‑oophorectomy in BRCA‑positive women
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling only high‑risk BRCA carriers limits generalizability to average‑risk population
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: No molecular stratification (e.g., STIC presence, interferon signature) to identify women who would benefit most
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Early enrollment (90 pts in part 1, 30 in part 2) likely underpowered for cancer incidence endpoints

key_data_gaps: No overall survival or long‑term cancer incidence data reported
No biomarker‑stratified analysis of STIC presence

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify molecular signatures (e.g., interferon signaling, STIC genomic alterations) that predict which high‑risk women will benefit most from delayed oophorectomy.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "the threat of future high‑grade serous peritoneal cancer is still considerable, upwards of 30% at 10 years" and highlighted lack of STIC‑specific risk assessment.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate hyperspectral imaging, ctDNA, and clinical data to generate a predictive risk score for post‑salpingectomy cancer development.

crispro_opportunity.description: Incorporate dynamic biomarkers (e.g., serial ctDNA, imaging‑derived STIC burden) into TUBAWISP‑2 to avoid dilution of signal.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted that current trial compares only quality‑of‑life and oncologic equivalence without molecular endpoints.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO’s longitudinal data platform can track on‑treatment biomarker trajectories and inform adaptive trial designs.

cited_competitors.name: UKCTOCS
cited_competitors.context: Cited as the largest ovarian cancer screening trial that showed no mortality benefit.

cited_competitors.name: BioView
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned as a clinically approved automated FISH scoring platform used for validation of Orion FISH.

unresolved_questions: What is the optimal age window for opportunistic salpingectomy in average‑risk women?
How should women with residual STIC after risk‑reducing surgery be managed (e.g., PARP inhibitors, vaccines)?

talk_metadata.talk_title: OVASEEK: hyperspectral imaging of whole fallopian tubes for STIC detection
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we can develop an instrument that can scan these and look at the whole fallopian tube in about 15 minutes"
Speaker said: "the first couple of fallopian tubes that we looked at took about eight hours to image"

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed that the system required custom lasers and optics, and current prototype takes ~8 hours per tube, indicating scalability and throughput limitations.
competitive_moat_weakness.scale_or_manufacturing_bottleneck: Long imaging time and need for specialized laser hardware hinder rapid clinical deployment.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Current workflow requires shipping tubes to MIT, breaking standard pathology chain of custody.

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker claimed the system could scan a whole tube in 15 minutes, but later admitted the first instruments required eight hours per tube — contradiction between promised speed and actual performance.

key_data_gaps: No prospective clinical outcome data linking OVASEEK findings to cancer incidence
Limited validation of fluorescence nanoparticle markers in diverse patient cohorts

crispro_opportunity.description: Apply CrisPRO’s computational spine to OVASEEK hyperspectral cubes to improve STIC detection accuracy and reduce imaging time through AI‑driven wavelength selection.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted 86% prediction accuracy for nanoparticle binding using current ML pipelines and the need for faster processing.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can optimize feature extraction and model inference to achieve real‑time analysis, enabling intra‑operative decision support.

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop imaging‑derived biomarkers (e.g., MYC vs cyclin E amplification signatures) that predict aggressive STIC phenotypes.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted polyclonal MYC and cyclin E copy‑number states within STIC lesions and differing immune microenvironments.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate imaging, spatial proteomics, and FISH data to generate composite risk scores for individual lesions.

cited_competitors.name: Cision Vision
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned as a commercialized lymph‑node imaging tool that informed the development of OVASEEK.

cited_competitors.name: BioView
cited_competitors.context: Used for validation of copy‑number calls in Orion FISH experiments.

unresolved_questions: How can imaging time be reduced from hours to clinically acceptable minutes?
Can the platform reliably differentiate aggressive vs indolent STICs without fluorescent labels?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Orion FISH platform and the Pre‑cancer Atlas of fallopian tubes
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "STIC lesions are heterogeneous… MYC amplified regions tended to be more associated with immune cells"
Speaker said: "we observed copy‑number gain in morphologically normal epithelium"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of clinical annotation linking specific copy‑number or interferon signatures to eventual peritoneal carcinomatosis

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Limited to 16‑18 protein markers and up to four DNA‑FISH probes per slide, constraining breadth of genomic interrogation.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Requires specialized imaging equipment and expertise, potentially limiting widespread adoption.

key_data_gaps: No prospective validation that MYC or cyclin E amplification patterns predict clinical outcomes
Limited number of normal‑appearing tubes examined for copy‑number alterations

crispro_opportunity.description: Use CrisPRO to integrate Orion FISH protein, DNA‑FISH, and spatial transcriptomics data to define a composite STIC aggressiveness score.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted polyclonal MYC and cyclin E copy‑number states and their distinct immune neighborhoods.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model multi‑omic spatial relationships and predict which STIC subclones are likely to progress.

crispro_opportunity.description: Incorporate Orion‑derived molecular endpoints into ongoing TUBAWISP‑2 and Protector UK trials to enrich for high‑risk STIC carriers.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted that current trials lack molecular stratification of STIC lesions.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can generate real‑time molecular readouts for adaptive randomization.

cited_competitors.name: BioView
cited_competitors.context: Used as an independent validation platform for copy‑number calls in Orion FISH experiments.

cited_competitors.name: Cision Vision
cited_competitors.context: Referenced as a related imaging technology for intra‑operative pathology.

unresolved_questions: What specific copy‑number or interferon signatures definitively predict progression from STIC to invasive disease?
Can the Orion platform be scaled to include additional oncogenic loci beyond MYC and cyclin E?
